daily_reality.typical_day: On job sites until 14:00. Back in office: checks emails, invoices, and tender portals when he remembers. Does everything himself — no dedicated BD person. Bids on 1-2 public tenders per month, mostly relies on Stammkunden (repeat clients) for private work.
daily_reality.biggest_time_waste: Forgets to check portals for days. Misses Submissionsfristen. When he does check, the portal UX is terrible on his phone.
daily_reality.weekly_volume: Checks portals 2-3x/week (inconsistently), finds 1-2 relevant tenders, bids on maybe 1 per month
decision_criteria: Auftragswert €50K-€500K (not too small, not too large)

psychology.tech_savviness: low — uses smartphone and basic apps but struggles with complex interfaces
psychology.risk_tolerance: low — sticks to what he knows, won't bid on unfamiliar project types
psychology.decision_speed: gut feeling — decides quickly based on experience, doesn't use formal scoring
delight_triggers: email alert: 'Neuer Auftrag in Ihrer Nähe'

first_screen: Simple list: here are tenders matching your trade and location
Each tender: title, location, value, deadline — nothing more
Big obvious button to see details
Works on my phone at the Baustelle
deal_breakers: Too many screens/clicks to see a tender
nice_to_have: VOB tips for each tender type
expectations.current_pain_level: 5/10 — not actively suffering but knows he's missing opportunities
